title: Contributing to animovement
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to animovement
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to animovement
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("roaldarbol/animovement", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to animovement
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to animovement
text: Please note animovement project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Introduction to animovement
text: get started, first install package. Next, put together tutorial takes necessary steps, reading data summarising movements. recommend going steps specified order recommended workflow. Read trackball data Clean tracks Compute kinematics Compute movement statistics

title: Calculate kinematics
text: work movement data, often interested just animal ; ’re interested fast moves, heading etc. calculate_kinematics calculates range kinematic variables: distance: distance animal moved since last observation (simply calculated using Pythagoras’ theorem) v_translation: translational velocity, like see speedometer car. direction: direction (radians) animal heading - arrow compass heading. rotation: Difference direction last observation. v_rotation: rotational velocity (rad/s).

title: Calculate kinematics
code: df_kinematics_clean <- df_kinematics |>   filter_na_speed(threshold = 100)

title: Clean tracks
text: case removed outliers, can now interpolate across gaps created. CONTINUED…

title: Clean tracks
text: left smooth tracks, done using smooth_tracks() function. smoothing super simple. smooth_tracks() provides different options: roll_mean roll_median SOON savitsky_golay rolling filters can provide window_width, .e. many observations use rolling filter. filters result NA values beginning end data. important point smooth_tracks() , instead using x y values, first back-transforms raw values obtained sensors (effectively “differences” coordinates, dx dy) performs smoothing , finally converting back x y. may seem strange previously worked tracking data computer vision GPS loggers. However, whereas modalities return “true” coordinates outlier, mouse sensors . way can identify rogue values filtering raw values. Let’s try smoothing data rolling_mean filter 0.5 second (30 observations sampling rate 60Hz) window width. case work multiple keypoints /individuals can use group_by metadata tidyverse-friendly workflow. Let’s visualise compare. Note although difference may seem negligible plotting paths, may become important computing derivatives velocity acceleration.  different sensors good job! can see smoothed track end bit left raw version.
code: df_rollmedian <- df |>   filter_aniframe(method = "rollmedian",                    window_width = 3,                    use_derivatives = TRUE) df_kalman <- df |>   filter_aniframe(method = "kalman",                    sampling_rate = 60,                    use_derivatives = TRUE) df_sgolay <- df |>   filter_aniframe(method = "sgolay",                    sampling_rate = 60,                    use_derivatives = TRUE) df_lowpass <- df |>   filter_aniframe(method = "lowpass",                    cutoff_freq = 0.1,                    sampling_rate = 60,                    use_derivatives = TRUE) library(ggplot2) ggplot() +   geom_path(data = df, aes(x, y), colour = "red") +   geom_path(data = df_rollmedian, aes(x, y), colour = "blue") +   geom_path(data = df_kalman, aes(x, y), colour = "orange") +   geom_path(data = df_sgolay, aes(x, y), colour = "purple") +   geom_path(data = df_lowpass, aes(x, y), colour = "green") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_path()`). #> Warning: Removed 19 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_path()`).

title: Analyse DeepLabCut data
text: however expect noise sampling process. Thus, may want filter data, commonly done movement data across fields, although preferred method differs fields. filter_ functions. can try different ones see difference. Importantly, data, since raw collected data changes timepoints absolute positions (*e.g. position image), need convert data back original state, filtering, convert back positions 2D; luckily, filter_aniframe can us, simply enabling use_derivatives = TRUE. finally happy data best possible quality, - - can move calculations based data.

title: Mission & Scope
text: primary goal animovement package offer streamlined standardised workflow analysing animal movement data, syntax designed integrate seamlessly tidyverse ecosystem. Animal movement, whether produced humans animals, crucial navigating interacting environment. Understanding movements great interest scientists diverse fields ethology, behavioral ecology, biomechanics, neuroscience. numerous tools available quantifying movement, data produce — whether video tracking software hardware like treadmills, trackballs, accelerometers — often lack standardised approach analysis. makes difficult easily process compare movement data across different studies platforms. animovement package addresses gap establishing standardised workflow processing movement data, leveraging common data formats (collaboration movement) offering “recipe” streamlined data analysis.

title: Mission & Scope
text: core, animovement processes trajectories individual keypoints time. spatial position individual represented one (centroid) keypoints (pose), provided 1D (x), 2D (x, y), 3D (x, y, z) coordinates. sequentially collected positions form tracks time. neuroscience ethology, tracks commonly generated : Pose estimation tools like DeepLabCut SLEAP, track multiple keypoints individual. Centroid tracking software like TRex idtracker.ai, focuses single point (centroid) per individual. Treadmills trackballs, record movement belt ball, serving proxies 1D (treadmill) 2D (trackball) centroid tracking. vision provide intuitive accessible workflow using familiar tidyverse syntax. animovement designed handle data various sources, supporting 1D, 2D, 3D tracking single multiple individuals. practice, goal make possible derive meaningful insights movement data fewer 10 lines code. example:
code: library(animovement) movement_summary <- read_deeplabcut(path) |>   clean_tracks() |>   calculate_kinematics() |>   clean_kinematics() |>   calculate_statistics()

title: Read trackball data
text: recommend keeping analysis scripts data project (doesn’t mean folder), use great package package manage files (see documentation learn ). First, ’ll let R know project root folder. data lives funky place (constraints R package), giving file paths easy. Great, now file paths. function needs know things. Takes either "free" "fixed". column contains time stamps either seconds datetime format (takes either column number name quotes, e.g. 4 "time") can actual sampling rate, use information bin observations, can easily choose lower sampling rate. value optional, provided used convert spatial estimates unit-less centimeters. Can found computer mice. Let’s try read provided files:
code: library(here, quietly = TRUE) #> here() starts at /home/runner/work/animovement/animovement here::i_am("vignettes/articles/read-trackball.Rmd") #> here() starts at /home/runner/work/animovement/animovement filepaths <- get_sample_data("trackball", quiet = TRUE)  # `get_sample_data()` downloads a pair of example sensor files. In your own # analysis these would be paths to your two sensor files, e.g. # c(here("data", "trackball", "sensor_1.csv"), here("data", "trackball", "sensor_2.csv")) df <- read_trackball(   filepaths,   setup = "of_free",   col_time = "datetime",   sampling_rate = 60 ) |>   # Trackball coordinates come out in sensor dots; calibrate to cm   # (394 dots per cm for this mouse sensor).   set_unit_space(to_unit = "cm", calibration_factor = 1 / 394)

title: Analyse movement across space and time
text: R toolbox analysing movement across space time primary aim animovement package provide unified, standardised workflow analysing movement data tidyverse-friendly syntax. work actively developers Python movement package, reach similar data standards, workflow use cases; prefer analysing data Python, highly recommend using movement.

title: Analyse movement across space and time
text: can install development version animovement : installed package, can load :
code: install.packages('animovement', repos = c('https://animovement.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org')) library("animovement")

title: The animovement — animovement
text: Functions extend reduce number packages animovement animovement_extend()animovement_detach() Function display conflicts animovement packages (attached packages) animovement_conflicts() Function update animovement packages (dependencies) install (missing) packages animovement_update()animovement_install() Utilities retrieve names animovement packages (dependencies), update status produce situation report. animovement_packages()animovement_deps()animovement_sitrep()

title: The animovement — animovement
text: options(animovement.quiet = TRUE) disable automatic messages (including conflict reporting) calling library(animovement), animovement_extend, animovement_update(install = TRUE) animovement_install. options(animovement.styling = FALSE) disable styling applied text printed console. options(animovement.extend = c(...)) can set calling library(animovement) extend animovement packages session. can done animovement_extend function library(animovement), also populate options("animovement.extend"). options(animovement.install = TRUE) can set library(animovement) install missing packages beforehand. See also animovement_install.

title: Conflicts between the animovement and other packages — animovement_conflicts
code: # Check conflicts between animovement packages and all attached packages animovement_conflicts()  # Check conflicts among all attached packages animovement_conflicts(sub("package:", "", search()[-1])) #> -- Conflicts ---------------------------------------- animovement_conflicts() -- #> x methods::body<-()    masks base::body<-() #> x methods::kronecker() masks base::kronecker()

code: animovement_deps(   pkg = animovement_packages(),   recursive = FALSE,   repos = getOption("repos"),   include.self = FALSE,   check.deps = TRUE )

title: List all animovement dependencies — animovement_deps
text: pkg character vector packages check dependencies versions . default animovement packages. recursive logical. TRUE recursively determines packages required operate packages. FALSE list packages direct dependencies. repos repositories use check updates. Defaults getOptions("repos"). include.self logical. TRUE also includes animovement package checks CRAN version. check.deps logical. FALSE determine dependencies display update status packages pkg.

title: List all animovement dependencies — animovement_deps
text: data frame giving package names, CRAN local version, logical variable stating whether local version behind CRAN version.

title: Detach animovement packages — animovement_detach
text: Detaches animovement packages, removing search path.

title: Detach animovement packages — animovement_detach
code: animovement_detach(   ...,   unload = FALSE,   force = FALSE,   include.self = TRUE,   session = FALSE )

title: Detach animovement packages — animovement_detach
text: ... comma-separated package names, quoted unquoted, vectors package names. left empty, packages returned animovement_packages detached. unload logical. TRUE also unloads packages using detach(name, unload = TRUE). force logical. animovement package detached / unloaded even though attached packages depend ? include.self logical. TRUE also includes animovement package - applicable ... left empty. session logical. TRUE also removes packages options("animovement.extend"), attached library(animovement) current session. ... left empty include.self = TRUE, clear animovement options set session.

title: Detach animovement packages — animovement_detach
text: animovement_detach returns NULL invisibly.

title: Extend the animovement — animovement_extend

title: Download all suggested packages — animovement_install_suggested
text: animovement, minimal dependency policy keep package light fast install. However, rely several packages testing specific features. "soft dependencies" can downloaded using function, allowing fully utilize animovement's functionalities without errors.

title: Download all suggested packages — animovement_install_suggested
text: reduce dependency load, animovement default download internally needed packages. ask user download needed. current function can help install packages animovement ecosystem packages might need. animovement_show_suggested() convenient helper show current list suggested packages. function check Suggests field animovement imported packages (aniframe, aniread, aniprocess, animetric, anicheck, anivis), excluding development packages (knitr, rmarkdown, testthat). package {pak} installed, pak::pkg_install() used install packages. Otherwise, utils::install.packages() used animovement R-universe repository.

title: animovement 0.5.1
text: Previously, smooth_track built smoothen trackball data. fix, fixed following: - Changed name smooth_movement - Added parameter use_derivatives (default: FALSE) trackball data smoothed - Written non-derivative code now smooth_movement able smooth movement data!

title: animovement 0.5.0
text: big update! three major updates: - finally stabilised data format implemented readers! - ’ve introduced metadata. ’s still experimental, important step right direction. - added tests ensure output data frames readers always contain expected columns column classes. - also added tests ensure metadata present reading data frame. nearly done work metadata, exited finally get started.

title: animovement 0.4.1
text: Changed parameter read_animalta().

title: animovement 0.4.0
text: Added readers AnimalTA (read_animalta) idtracker.ai (read_idtracker).

title: animovement 0.3.0
text: added ability read centroid tracking Bonsai files read_bonsai().

title: animovement 0.2.0
text: Package name changed animovement! reflect change scope package now cover wide variety movement data, package changed name. conscious decision maintaining two packages similar functionality wouldn’t feasible long-term. new update brings wealth new functionality, also lot breaking changes. update, package aligns much closer vision , expect major breaking changes near future. let’s delve new stuff! Much improved documentation Added new readers read_deeplabcut(), read_sleap(), read_trex()… Standardised data cleaning smooth_tracks()  tests Although annoying current users, necessity ensure proper testing various functions now broken smaller subsets. still need former trackballr package functionality, can download source files Github install source. encounter bugs, please report Github issues.
